capabilities.claim: Contract, Preflight, verification, Summary, archive, PR, close, and base-sync governance exists in this repository.
The template can inspect project facts and create a profile proposal; it does not prove completed adopter calibration.
An adopter receives the governed entrypoints when the installer completes; installed availability must be checked in the adopter repository.
The Runtime provides an adopter-executable, Japanese-default ten-stage calibration Session with pause/resume, review, checks, evidence, and fail-closed Candidate activation.
Calibration Candidate activation is atomic and preserves the previous Active configuration when activation fails.
Interactive Bootstrap lifecycle states and schema are not represented as a completed capability in the current Runtime.
capabilities.missingEvidence: Existing wizard helpers do not yet prove the planned Bootstrap lifecycle schema and readiness boundary.

capabilities.claim: A complete per-file Ownership Manifest and managed-region lifecycle is not yet proven for all update/rollback/uninstall paths.
capabilities.missingEvidence: No complete adopter evidence chain covers project modification, drift, rollback, disable/enable, and uninstall for every ownership class.

capabilities.claim: Proposal/apply and uninstall evidence boundaries exist in template code and documentation, but adopter installation and standard Work Item integration remain to be completed.
Quick Install does not yet default to independently verified Release Archive SHA256 evidence.
capabilities.missingEvidence: A source-bound release archive digest check and clean/anonymous install output are not yet recorded.

capabilities.claim: Release and PR claims require independently bound Workflow Run, Head SHA, Job Conclusion, and artifact evidence before being called verified.
capabilities.missingEvidence: The repository does not yet maintain a canonical source-bound CI/Release Evidence record for every published claim.

capabilities.claim: Enterprise identity, authorization, isolation, immutable audit, and compliance assurance remain outside AI Cockpit evidence.
